memoryArtifacts.headline: Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow於1310年出生在蘇格蘭維托史爾吉萊斯皮時，他的父親Colin Callen Oig Campbell30歲，母親Helena Lennox Menteith25歲。他於1342年5月2日與Mary Isabella Lamont在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾被列為sheriff。他去世於1372年在他的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow于1310年出生在苏格兰維托史爾吉萊斯皮时，他的父亲Colin Callen Oig Campbell30岁，母亲Helena Lennox Menteith25岁。他于1342年5月2日与Mary Isabella Lamont在英国苏格兰阿吉爾 安德 布特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他在苏格兰艾斯爾艾爾被列为sheriff。他去世于1372年在他的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾洛霍。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Archibald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman French origin, from a Germanic (Frankish) personal name derived from ercan ‘genuine’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. It has long been associated with Scotland, where it is in regular use as the English equivalent of Gaelic Gilleasbaig ( see Gillespie ).

variants.name: Archibold
meanings: Of Norman French origin, from a Germanic (Frankish) personal name derived from ercan ‘genuine’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. It has long been associated with Scotland, where it is in regular use as the English equivalent of Gaelic Gilleasbaig ( see Gillespie ).

nameMeanings.name: Mor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow was born in 1310, in Gillespie, Wigtownshire, Scotland, his father, Colin Callen Oig Campbell, was 30 and his mother, Helena Lennox Menteith, was 25. He married Mary Isabella Lamont on 2 May 1342, in Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as sheriff in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. He died in 1372, in his hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow est né en 1310, à Gillespie, Wigtownshire, Écosse, son père, Colin Callen Oig Campbell, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Helena Lennox Menteith, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Mary Isabella Lamont le 2 mai 1342, à Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse. Il est décédé en 1372, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow 1310 in Gillespie, Wigtownshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Colin Callen Oig Campbell 30 und seine Mutter Helena Lennox Menteith 25. Er hat am 2. Mai 1342 in Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom Mary Isabella Lamont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als sheriff in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb 1372 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow è nato il nel 1310 nel Gillespie, Wigtownshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Colin Callen Oig Campbell, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Helena Lennox Menteith, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Mary Isabella Lamont il 2 maggio 1342, nel Argyll and Bute, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Morì nel 1372, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochowが1310年にスコットランドウィグトウンシュアイアギレスパで生まれた時，父Colin Callen Oig Campbellは30歳，母Helena Lennox Menteithは25歳であった。1342年5月2日にイギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトでMary Isabella Lamontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。スコットランドエイルシュアイアエイルでの職業はsheriffと記録されている。彼は1372年に故郷で62歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルロチャウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow(이)가 1310년 스컷랜드 위그타운실르 길레스피에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Colin Callen Oig Campbell(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Helena Lennox Menteith(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1342년 5월 2일 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트에서 Mary Isabella Lamont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 sheriff이었다. 그는 1372년 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 럭아베에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow nasceu em 1310, em Gillespie, Wigtownshire, Scotland, seu pai, Colin Callen Oig Campbell, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Helena Lennox Menteith, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Isabella Lamont em 2 de maio de 1342, em Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff em Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Ele faleceu em 1372, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow родился в 1310 г. в местности джиллеспи, вигтоуншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Colin Callen Oig Campbell было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Helena Lennox Menteith, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Isabella Lamont 2 мая 1342 г. в местности аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии sheriff в местности аир, аиршир, Шотландия. Он умер в 1372 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Archibald Mor Campbell 12th. Laird of Lochow nació en 1310, en Gillespie, Wigtownshire, Scotland, su papá, Colin Callen Oig Campbell, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Helena Lennox Menteith, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Mary Isabella Lamont el 2 de mayo de 1342, en Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff en Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Murió en 1372, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland.
